title: Six colours that say nothing
checkLabels.t7: The hero has a colour underneath
why.empty: The editor is empty. Hit the bin and start over.
why.untouched: The code is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: Six fixes. Each one comes from the last six lessons.
why.unlinked: The page cannot find styles.css. You changed the link line.
hint2.unlinked: Hit the bin and start over.
why.used-inline: You used style in the tag. That is lesson 01 again.
why.touched-html: You edited the HTML. It is correct — the mistakes are in the styles.
why.no-hero: There is no element with the hero class.
why.no-badge: There is no element with the badge class.
why.no-brand: There is no element with the brand class.
why.no-card: There is no element with the card class.
why.no-muted: There is no element with the muted class.
hint2.shorthand-left: It resets everything it does not mention — including the colour on the line above it.
why.hero-wiped: There is no colour under the hero image.
hint2.hero-wiped: The colour is set, but the next line erases it. Use the property for the image only.
hint2.badge-flat: A gradient is an image, and it has been put in the property for a colour. So the value is invalid and gets thrown away.
why.brand-missing: The brand line is not brown.
hint2.brand-missing: You are calling a variable by a name that does not exist. Check what it is called in :root.
hint3.brand-missing: There is a detail here that surprises people: when the variable is missing, the value becomes invalid AFTER the cascade. The browser does not fall back to an earlier rule; it takes the inherited value instead. That is why the text is not the default black but the colour of its parent.
hint2.border-gone: Look at the numbers in its colour. One of them has no unit.
hint3.border-gone: Saturation and lightness in hsl are written as percentages. Without the sign the value cannot be read and the whole declaration is dropped — border and all.
why.hard-shadow: The shadow is hard and black.
why.muted-pale: The note is in too pale a grey.
hint2.muted-pale: On white that falls below the required ratio. The threshold for normal text is 4.5 to 1.
blocks.0.text: The page is finished and almost none of the colours work. The console is clean. Every rule is written without a syntax error, and half the values never reach the screen.
blocks.2.text: Here is what was intended. Each of the six is a mistake from the last six lessons:
blocks.3.items.0: The hero has a dark brown colour under the image so white text is never left over nothing.
blocks.3.items.1: The NEW badge fades from brown to dark brown.
blocks.3.items.2: The brand line is in the brand brown.
blocks.3.items.3: The card has a thin brown border.
blocks.3.items.4: Its shadow is soft, made of two layers.
blocks.3.items.5: The note is quieter than the text but readable.
blocks.4.text: An invalid value is not an error to the browser. It is a line it simply steps over.
blocks.6.text: Three questions, in this order. First: is the value valid at all — a missing percent sign, an image in a colour property, a variable name that does not exist. Second: is something below erasing it — shorthands reset everything they do not mention. Third: if the value does reach the screen, does it have enough contrast.
blocks.8.text: Fix the six things in styles.css only. Do not touch the HTML — it is correct.
